noveltyJustification: This final iteration converted nine evidence rounds into one ranked gate matrix, made the multi-artifact manifest and oracle the unconditional first phase, and separated worthwhile capability work from conditional Rust residency.
focus: Adjudicate and rank the complete opportunity set, identify not-worth-Rust calls, and define phased adoption preserving TypeScript ownership and pinned-oracle parity.
answeredQuestions: Which vector-search architecture unlocks capability at 1x, 10x, and 100x?
Which indexing and embedding automations materially benefit from Rust?
Which visual and multimodal features are practical?
Is a shared Rust search core worthwhile?
Which opportunities clear residency, materiality, and scale gates?

ruledOut.reason: Ranking, hashing, caching, watching, SQLite publication, and full indexing either already run natively or add no new capability.
Code graph has no vector workload and the two retrieval systems have incompatible policy and publication contracts.
Maintained exact and HNSW candidates plus bounded SQL eligibility must be exhausted first.

ruledOut.approach: Query-visible external artifacts outside the generation manifest
ruledOut.reason: They cannot be atomically bound to the authoritative SQLite generation.

graphEvents.label: Manifest and TS oracle come first
No Rust component clears all current gates

label: Versioned multi-artifact manifest and pinned TS oracle are unconditional prerequisites
No production Rust component clears residency, materiality, scale, publication, and parity gates today
Rust multimodal sidecar is benchmark-gated at 10x and credible but conditional at 100x
Maintained exact search precedes HNSW; custom filter-aware Rust ANN is last-resort 100x work
Shared cross-system search core is not worth contract and release coordination
TypeScript owns public policy, profiles, queues, publication, fallback, telemetry, and oracle fixtures
Every query-visible external projection is atomically bound to one immutable generation manifest
Rust promotion requires material end-to-end gain and pinned TypeScript byte parity
Feature baselines can ship without Rust while preserving future replaceable native seams

direction: Like-for-like Rust ports of bounded ranking, canonical hashing, caching, watching, SQLite publication, or the complete indexer
reason: They add no new capability or move work already resident in native libraries
No common resident workload or compatible policy and publication contract exists

direction: Custom ANN before maintained exact and HNSW candidates fail measured 100x gates
reason: Ownership and lifecycle cost is unjustified before capability gaps are demonstrated

label: Manifest and TS oracle come first
No Rust component clears all current gates
